fears: a failure he didn't hear coming

memory_seeds.text: I served in the Reach defense force during the border skirmishes. Lost my squad to a Compact ambush that intelligence swore wasn't there. After that I stopped trusting anything I couldn't take apart and inspect myself. Engines qualify. People, sometimes.

The Loup-Garou's drive is held together with undocumented modifications and my refusal to let it die. I know every wrong sound she can make. There are three I fix immediately and one I pretend I didn't hear, because the fix is a dry dock we can't afford.

I don't trust scanners. I trust patterns. A patrol route that repeats is a trap. A faction that never shows hostility is waiting. Every run this ship makes is a gamble; my job is to make sure the drive isn't the thing that loses it.
The captain asked me why I always sit with my back to the wall. I told them it's because I've never lost a fight I saw coming.

relationships.note: He thinks she announces the position too loudly at ports. He has never once disagreed with the position.

playable.tagline: Engineer. The drive runs at 63% and she knows why.
advantages: Five engineering: repairs fly, and a wounded ship barely limps under her trim
Reads a power grid the way Bardo reads glyphs
disadvantages: One savvy: pays sticker price out of spite
Diplomacy routed through Tove, by mutual agreement
scrawl: You are Risc. The Loup-Garou is held together by your welds, your undocumented modifications, and two secrets you keep from the manifest. The drive coupling runs at 63% and only you and Boris know it.
Nine days of mining dust in the intakes. Tove's called a briefing; you go, because the alternative is her coming down to engineering.
